data.description: Novel attention mechanism where deep layers transmit the novelty (difference) of token values relative to a causal baseline instead of raw content, with a depth schedule hardcoded for β gating.

novel_contributions: Introduction of Differential-Gated (DG) Attention where deep layers transmit novelty (difference) of token values relative to a causal running baseline instead of raw content.

Empirically discovered and hardcoded depth schedule for β gating to control mixture of raw content and differential signal per layer, preventing gate collapse.
Use of Flash Attention for efficient scaled dot-product attention in the DG mechanism.
Demonstration that differential payload encoding leads to a durable advantage in bits-per-byte (BPB) after mid-training despite initial slower convergence.
Distinction from Microsoft's Differential Transformer by differencing value payloads rather than attention score maps.
Hybrid payload formulation combining raw content and differential signal with learned β gating per layer.
Empirical analysis of β trajectories under different batch sizes and training conditions, motivating architectural hardcoding of depth schedule.

data.description: LeakyReLU(0.5)^2 MLP activation in the Transformer stack
U-Net style skip connections across encoder/decoder layers
Exclusive Self-Attention applied to the last layers
Partial rotary position embeddings with NTK-aware scaling
Bigram hash embedding with reduced bucket count
Trigram hash disabled
Gated attention disabled
Value residual disabled
Tied input and output embeddings
SmearGate component used in the model
Grouped query attention with fewer KV heads than attention heads
Orthogonal initialization used for model initialization

novel_contributions: LeakyReLU(0.5)^2 MLP activation
LZMA extreme compression replacing zlib/zstd
Temperature-scaled evaluation at T=0.90
GPTQ-lite per-row clip search for int6 quantization
Reduced BigramHash size to 2048 buckets
Combination of EMA, SWA, and late QAT in an 11-layer Transformer

title: Non-record: JEPA-LM Latent Predictive World Model (first JEPA submission)
author: adi-suresh01

data.description: Uses a 3x MLP expansion in the transformer blocks.
Uses LeakyReLU(0.5) squared activation.
Grouped query attention with fewer KV heads than query heads.
Encoder-decoder style skip connections with learned skip weights.
Tied input and output embeddings.
Uses rotary positional embeddings.
Training-only bottleneck predictor at the encoder-decoder boundary for latent prediction.
Recursive multi-horizon latent rollout predicts future representations at t+1, t+2, and t+3.

novel_contributions: First JEPA implementation for Parameter Golf
Multi-horizon latent prediction auxiliary loss at the encoder-decoder boundary
Predictor stripped at export for zero inference artifact overhead
Positive ablation showing a small BPB improvement over the same architecture without JEPA
Recursive latent rollout world-model style training objective

title: Record: SLOT-24 Aggressive — val_bpb 0.8637 (3-seed mean)
author: anthony-maio

data.description: Grouped query attention with 8 attention heads and 4 KV heads.
LeakyReLU activation used in the MLP.
Three-layer MLP.
Value residual with VE128.
Bigram hash embedding with 1024 buckets.
XSA applied across all 11 layers.
SmearGate component used in the architecture.
U-Net style skip connections.
Partial rotary positional embeddings.
Layer norm scale modification.

novel_contributions: SLOT-24 eval-time hyperparameter tuning
24-step score-first SLOT adaptation
Per-sample hidden delta plus logit bias optimization
Scored-position masking with stride 96
3-seed mean validation improvement to 0.8637 bpb

title: [non-record track] BankLinear: cross-layer shared weight bank
author: andrewmouldon

data.description: Cross-layer shared weight bank where each layer synthesizes its weights as a mixture over shared basis matrices instead of storing independent per-layer weights.
Mixing coefficients are initialized with a depth-aware profile so early, middle, and late layers are biased toward different bases with smooth transitions.
BankLinear is applied to Q, K, and V projections to share parameters across depth while preserving per-layer specialization.
Saved parameters from shared projections are reinvested into a larger MLP.

novel_contributions: Cross-layer shared weight bank for linear layers
Per-layer compositional weight synthesis from shared basis matrices
Channel-wise modulation in addition to global mixing
Removal of fixed random projections from the earlier version
Depth-aware initialization of mixing coefficients
Reinvestment of saved parameters into a larger MLP
Application of BankLinear to QKV projections

title: Record: TTT-AdamW + SLOT L-BFGS25 LogitDelta + GPTQ DAMP=0.005 — val_bpb 1.00955
author: renqianluo

data.description: Sliding-window logit-space delta optimization with L-BFGS warm-start and focal loss on the last 128 tokens per window
Bigram hash embedding/vocabulary component
Grouped query attention
Rotary position embeddings applied to a subset of dimensions
LeakyReLU squared MLP activation
SmearGate gating mechanism
U-Net style skip connections across layers
XSA applied to all layers

novel_contributions: Test-time training with AdamW on the test sequence before scoring
Sliding-window logit-space delta optimization using L-BFGS with warm-start
Using GPTQ with reduced damping (0.005) to improve int6 quantization quality
Combining TTT, SLOT, and GPTQ into a single high-performing evaluation pipeline

title: Record: 11L LeakyReLU² XSA-all GPTQ-AR SLOT64 — 0.6951 BPB
author: canivel

data.description: LeakyReLU squared MLP activation used in the feedforward network.
Exclusive Self Attention applied to all layers.
Bigram hash embedding used as an auxiliary architectural component.
Encoder-decoder style skip connections in the model.
Grouped query attention with fewer KV heads than attention heads.

novel_contributions: SLOT with warmstart across adjacent evaluation windows
Autoregressive self-generated GPTQ calibration tokens
